channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: NCCIA asks Supreme Court to withdraw order requiring quick decision on Imaan Mazari and Hadi Chattha sentence suspension pleas
item.description: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has approached the Supreme Court seeking withdrawal of its May 12 order that directed the Islamabad High Court (IHC) to decide within two weeks th...

item.title: Decline of the White Picket Fence Reflects Changing American Neighborhoods
item.description: Once a hallmark of middle-class neighborliness and the quintessential symbol of the American Dream, the white picket fence is steadily disappearing from American front yards. In Southington, Connectic...

item.title: NCCIA approaches Supreme Court seeking withdrawal of order directing IHC on sentence suspension pleas of Imaan Mazari and Hadi Chattha
item.description: The National Cyber Crime Investigation Agency (NCCIA) has approached the Supreme Court of Pakistan seeking withdrawal of its earlier order that directed the Islamabad High Court (IHC) to decide the se...

item.title: Escalation of Conflict Between Israel and Hezbollah Hits Beirut and Northern Israel
item.description: Tensions between Israel and Hezbollah have escalated as Israeli forces carried out airstrikes on the southern suburbs of Beirut, following rocket attacks on northern Israel. Israeli Prime Minister Ben...

item.title: Understanding free bets in online betting and the conditions behind them
item.description: The article explains what free bets are in online betting and why they appear attractive to many users, while also breaking down the hidden conditions that often come with them. Free bets are promotio...

item.title: Retired magistrate in Sibanyoni extortion case unlikely to face disciplinary action
item.description: The magistrate who previously presided over the high-profile extortion case involving Joe “Ferrari” Sibanyoni and his co-accused may no longer face any disciplinary consequences following her retireme...

item.title: Concerns grow over water pollution risks from AI data centers amid weak PFAS oversight in the U.S.
item.description: The article examines growing environmental concerns surrounding the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence data centers in the United States, particularly in Virginia, where large facilities requi...

item.title: Pakistan sets 4pc growth target and 8.2pc inflation for FY2026–27 amid economic stabilisation efforts
item.description: Islamabad reports that Pakistan’s government has set an economic growth target of 4 percent for FY2026–27, following a recorded GDP growth of 3.7 percent in FY2025–26, slightly below last year’s targe...

item.title: Comparing global tree estimates with Milky Way star counts highlights differences in how both are measured
item.description: This article examines the popular claim that there are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way and explains the scientific context behind both figures. According to widely cited research, Eart...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada says plunder case is politically motivated amid Senate debates
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada has publicly stated that he will not be intimidated by any threats related to his ongoing plunder case. Estrada claims that the charges filed against him are linked to politica...

item.title: Naira stabilises around ₦1,373 per dollar as CBN FX market shows steady trading on June 1, 2026
item.description: The Nigerian foreign exchange market opened on June 1, 2026 with the naira showing relative stability against the United States dollar, according to the latest data from official market sources. The C...

item.title: Historic Photograph of Buffalo Bill and Pawnee Leaders From 1885 Wild West Show
item.description: This historic photograph, taken around 1885, captures William F. “Buffalo Bill” Cody with a delegation of Pawnee scouts and chiefs during a tour of his world-famous Buffalo Bill’s Wild West show. By t...

item.title: Akwa Ibom TSA policy raises IGR from N7.5bn to N12bn within three months
item.description: The Akwa Ibom State Government says its introduction of the Treasury Single Account (TSA) has significantly improved the state’s Internally Generated Revenue (IGR), pushing it from N7.5 billion in the...

item.title: Public Opinions on Congress Leadership and Ramachandra Guha’s Analysis
item.description: The article gathers readers’ comments on historian Ramachandra Guha’s piece, which argues that the Congress party lacks competent leadership. Many readers agree with Guha, lamenting the party’s over-r...

item.title: Experts raise concerns over potential SpaceX index inclusion and impacts on retirement funds
item.description: Financial commentators and market analysts have raised concerns about the potential inclusion of Elon Musk’s SpaceX in major stock market indexes, warning that such a move could have significant impli...

item.title: PDEA conducts separate anti-drug operations, seizes P13.6 million worth of shabu in Mindanao
item.description: The Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA), together with local police units, carried out two separate anti-drug operations in Mindanao that resulted in the seizure of around two kilograms of suspe...

item.title: Wike Says Sandy Onor Is PDP’s Sole Presidential Candidate Amid Party Faction Dispute
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Nyesom Wike, has declared that Senator Sandy Onor is the only recognised presidential candidate of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) ahead of the 2027 g...

item.title: Stellantis limits JLR and Tata partnerships to US and India markets, with Europe not currently included
item.description: Stellantis has confirmed that it does not currently plan to extend its recently announced partnerships with Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) and Tata Motors into the European market. The company’s Europe chief...

item.title: Details of armoured military vehicle used during President Ruto’s Wajir Madaraka Day visit
item.description: During Kenya’s Madaraka Day celebrations held in Wajir County, a high-security operation drew attention after a specialised armoured military vehicle was deployed as part of President William Ruto’s s...

item.title: Armed groups demand N150m ransom after abducting Emir of Yashikira’s wives, threaten forced marriages
item.description: Fresh tension don enter Yashikira community in Kaiama Local Government Area of Kwara State after armed terrorists who kidnapped wives of the Emir and other residents reportedly escalate their demands ...
